messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: Good morning. What brings you into the clinic today?

messages.speaker: Patient
messages.text: I have had a throat pain that is sudden and severe since yesterday, and I am running a fever.

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: Let me check your vitals first. Your temperature is 101.3°F, blood pressure is 120/80 mmHg, oxygen saturation is 98%, and your weight is 165 lbs.

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: I see. Are you having any pain and discomfort while swallowing?

messages.speaker: Patient
messages.text: Yes, it is very painful to swallow, and I have swollen, tender lymph nodes in my neck.

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: Let me take a look. Yes, your tonsils are red and swollen, sometimes with white spots or streaks, which is a common symptom. I am going to do a rapid swab to test for the bacteria.

messages.speaker: Patient
messages.text: Okay. I don't have a cough or runny nose, though.

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: That actually makes strep more likely. The rapid test just came back positive.

messages.speaker: Patient
messages.text: What do I need to do to treat it?

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: I am prescribing oral amoxicillin for 10 days. You can also take over-the-counter Ibuprofen to help with the fever.

messages.speaker: Doctor
messages.text: Get plenty of rest and drink lots of fluids. You can return to regular activities 24 hours after taking your first dose of an antibiotic if you don't have a fever. Call the office for a follow-up if your symptoms are not improving after a couple of days.
